market_context: US stock futures are slipping modestly with Dow futures down 0.1%, S&P 500 futures down 0.1%, and Nasdaq 100 futures down 0.2% amid surging oil prices (Brent at $114.86, up nearly 7%) due to West Asia conflicts and inflation fears from strong producer prices data. The Dow hit a 2026 low yesterday, falling 1.6% below its 200-day moving average, heightening stagflation concerns and reducing Fed rate-cut odds. Markets await today's FOMC decision, dot plot, and Powell press conference, with rates likely holding at 3.50-3.75%.[1][2][3]

predictions.reasoning: S&P 500 E-Mini futures (ESH26) are down 0.18-0.79% pre-market, signaling a weak open after yesterday's 1.4% drop and Dow's 2026 low below 200-day MA. Oil spike to $114.86 and inflation data reduce Fed cut odds to 52%, pressuring broad indices lower.[1][2]

Nasdaq 100 E-Mini futures (NQH26) down 0.27% pre-market, extending yesterday's 1.5% Nasdaq decline amid stagflation fears from oil surge and geopolitics. Tech stocks like NVDA (-1.97%) and MU (-5.89%) show pre-market weakness.[1][2]

Brent crude spiked nearly 7% to $114.86 after West Asia energy attacks, driving commodity strength with S&P GSCI up 24.83% in 1-month. Energy ETFs like USO poised to extend gains amid ongoing tensions.[1][2]

Dow futures down 0.1% or 55 points pre-market after 768-point (1.6%) plunge to 2026 low yesterday, breaching 200-day MA on inflation and oil risks. Basic resources and miners down sharply on input costs.[1]
